preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.19.4: Three hardenings landing together to prevent the 2026-04-29 trypog.co WebFetch hang from recurring + clean up the misleading Meta-token error chain. (1) Brand-URL routing rule — commands/merlin.md now hard-pushes 'go to my site' / 'rescrape <url>' / 'check products on <url>' intents to mcp__merlin__brand_scrape (which has the 5/15/90s layered timeouts from CLAUDE.md Rule 13). The thin always-loaded router teaches the LLM to never WebFetch the user's own brand URL — built-in WebFetch has no enforced timeout and hung for 5+ minutes on a slow CDN. Universal rule added: WebFetch is a last-resort tool, used only when no MCP tool covers the intent; pre-fetch tells the user 'this can hang, hit Cancel if it stalls.' (2) Cancel button on the chat-status row — fades in at 30s of stuck status, calls the new abort-active-query IPC which interrupt()s the active SDK turn, drains the pending message queue, clears pending tool approvals, and broadcasts query-aborted so the renderer can clear the spinner + show a 'Cancelled' toast. Re-uses the same activeQuery.interrupt() machinery the brand-switch handler has used since v1.18.x. Means future hung tools are recoverable in one click instead of force-quitting Merlin. (3) Meta empty-token error message hardening — five fatal() sites in meta.go (2331, 2841, 3587, 3782, 4033) all said 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again,' which is exactly what the user had JUST done via the right-click 'Use my API key' modal in the trust-loss incident. New tokenMissingDiag helper (token_required.go) builds context-aware messages: brand-context → 'token saved correctly but invisible to this code path, restart Merlin and retry'; no-brand-context → 'connect Meta via the Magic panel tile, or paste a long-lived token via right-click "Use my API key".' 4 unit tests pin both cases and assert the pre-fix misleading hint can never reappear. Audit list for extending to tiktok/google/linkedin/amazon kept inline in token_required.go for follow-up. v1.19.3: HOTFIX — Meta manual-token modal: pasting an access token via right-click → 'Use my API key' on a brand-scoped tile (e.g. POG) silently failed with 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again', even though the token was correctly persisted to the brand vault. Root cause: ipcMain.handle('discover-meta-ids') in app/main.js spawned the Go binary with --config <global-config> only — the binary's loadConfig does NOT auto-merge .merlin-config-<brand>.json from disk, so the @@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@ placeholder (which the renderer wrote to the brand config file) never made it into the binary's Config struct, applyBrandVault had nothing to resolve, and cfg.MetaAccessToken stayed empty. Dashboard refresh works because it builds a strict brand-merged config in a temp file via buildStrictBrandConfig and passes THAT as --config (line ~6253). discoverMetaIds now mirrors the dashboard pattern exactly — temp config, brand-merged, deleted on completion (no decrypted credentials left on disk). REGRESSION GUARD comment added at the fix site documenting the live incident (brand=pog, 2026-04-28, screenshot in chat). v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. The IPC backend stays shipped, refactored into a reusable helper that's exposed to the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense. | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: Fixed: any fal.ai model under any namespace now works — pass openai/gpt-image-2/edit, black-forest-labs/flux-pro, or any future-lab/model/v1 and Merlin sends it through unchanged. Previously the binary auto-prepended fal-ai/ to every slash-containing slug, corrupting non-fal-ai/ namespaces into 404s. Live incident: openai/gpt-image-2/edit was unreachable.

New: Cancel button on the chat status row. When a tool call hangs (slow website fetch, third-party API stall), a Cancel button fades in next to the spinner after 30s — one click stops the turn. Previously the only escape was force-quitting Merlin.

Fixed: 'go to my site and check products' / 'rescrape <url>' now reliably routes to the brand-scrape tool (which has hardened 5/15/90s timeouts) instead of the built-in WebFetch (which has no timeout). Live incident: trypog.co rescrape hung the chat for 5+ minutes.

Fixed: when Merlin can't find a Meta access token, the error now says exactly what to do — 'restart Merlin and retry' for brand-scoped tiles, or 'connect Meta via the Magic panel' for global. Previously the same five fatal sites all said 'paste your token and run this again' — which loops the user into the same failure when the actual bug was upstream.

Fixed: pasting a Meta access token via the right-click → 'Use my API key' modal on a brand-scoped tile now works end-to-end. Previously the discover step failed with 'metaAccessToken required' even though the token was correctly saved — the binary spawn passed only the global config, missing the brand's vault placeholder. Now uses the same brand-merged temp config the dashboard uses.

Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
